details: DOI matches the Weng 2020 Pharmaceutics paper; the DSC discussion states an onset at 195 C for the ITR-TER cocrystal phase from slurring/slurry samples, with no conversion required.

The cited file is the imepitoin polymorphism paper in Molecules 2024, 29, 1724 with DOI 10.3390/molecules29081724, while the row cites Pharmaceutics 2024, 16, 496 and DOI 10.3390/pharmaceutics16040496.
DOI matches the Weng 2020 Pharmaceutics paper; the DSC analysis identifies TER as terephthalic acid and states it melted with sublimation at around 350 C.
DOI matches the Russ. J. Org. Chem. paper; the experimental characterization for compound 1b gives the named compound with mp 254 C (decomp.), matching the row.
DOI matches the Molecules 2023 paper; Table 2 lists [Glu(Osec-Bu)2][HCl] with Tonset 181.5 C, defined as the onset temperature of thermal degradation.
DOI matches the Molecules 2024 paper; the compound 35 characterization gives the full named compound and m.p. 120-122 C (dec.), whose midpoint is 121.0 C.
DOI matches the Molecules 2023 paper; Table 2 lists [Glu(Opr)2][HCl] with Tmax 193.4 C, defined as the temperature of fastest decomposition.
DOI matches the Molecules 2020 paper; Table 3 lists the [Ch][Ib]/[Ch][2mb] eutectic mixture with melting point 54 C and composition 0.51/0.49.
DOI matches the Adv. Pharm. Bull. paper; Table 2 lists Piroxicam-Nicotinamide with cocrystal melting point (1:1) 162-165 C, midpoint 163.5 C.
DOI matches the Nanomaterials 2024 paper; Table 1 lists CaCl2.6H2O with melting temperature 28 C in the representative hydrated salt PCM table.
